---
layout: default
title: "Political Maneuvering and Legislative Developments in India"
date: 2026-07-18T05:39:38.787049+00:00
summary: "The BJP pursues a two-thirds parliamentary majority via NCP reunification, while Parliament passes transgender rights legislation amid ongoing policy debates"
run_time_ist: "11:09AM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 11:09AM</p>


<h2 class="section-title">India National Politics</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>BJP’s parliamentary strategy:</strong> The BJP is actively working to reunite the NCP in Maharashtra as part of a broader effort to secure a two-thirds majority in Parliament.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I1</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I10</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Legislative outcomes:</strong> Parliament has successfully passed the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, even as a separate Constitutional Amendment Bill for women’s reservation failed to clear the Lok Sabha.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiuwFBVV95cUxOS1Y3RmZzYUVKcWZEU082Q3J2S210X3k5NEVLdmVkbTY2TlBsNFZIR2FoWFBrQTFaMDYwcXE3clIzTDN4VV83US1LUmxwd2FVeVZsNW5YeVZHU0ZMUnJLZjhTVjBnRFE2Y2ZpZTMtOWVqYU1TX3BNTHRvZjhscE1yT1JsRjVFYnFITm9GbVVkNEdkVjFrSTNvYldVUU1zSjlGaVZoZDE3T0x1Vmx5VW5ONnNvc0xxQU5kTlkw?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I2</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxPSTVKRHVGUXlhNjVqNEczYkNpd1NvbmRYOU5Fd2RIZjV3OHBob0owSzh3LVRXdUVqSDNVZTgtbVZlUG8ybS1wallfSVdTeTRtNENIU0lwV3piczdxV2NEVmE0WC1GSTQyMjR4dkNPb1pqblB2SzNHQjZBRWVXZHpPZXE2bVlQc3NGNEFJVU1NUHJMMFFqSkdRUVFFMGZNRUkzSTc0LXphM1JaLXM?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I7</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Electoral oversight:</strong> The Supreme Court has clarified that the Election Commission cannot equate electoral roll deletions with a loss of citizenship, while the ECI continues to emphasize fact-based reporting with media outlets.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I3</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I4</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiwFBVV95cUxQbHRpcFZ1VE1QcEJsTUVUMFhlNU9oN3B2V1dCay1id0FKRHkxVmxHdFJpNmxJVmtpRU5QWkE4ZXg5NVhTQXU0UHg3aU05WTVQbUlrZmJFSDY3b2lFVGF6bWpFMW0tRWh2eVhmLW9SbHhPVUNQNWVQVXZVQ1UxUW5wWTA2dWZENndPa3Qw?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I8</a></span></li>
</ul>

<h2 class="section-title">Policy and Institutions</h2>

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Judicial and administrative challenges:</strong> The Kerala Waqf Board has approached the Supreme Court to challenge a High Court order that restricts its decision-making authority.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.-?oc=5&amp;hl=en-CA&amp;gl=CA&amp;ceid=CA%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W2</a></span></li>
</ul>
